metadata.title: Build a Post-Build Learning Document Generator with Codex

beats.narration_text: The build experience turns into durable skill only if you capture what was learned immediately after — most builders skip that step.

The post-build learning document is the Feynman test applied to your own build: if you can't write specifically what you learned and what you'd change, you borrowed the capability from Codex instead of building it yourself. The five-section format forces specificity — not 'learned about database design' but the exact Codex behavior discovered.

claude "Generate the 'What I learned I didn't know before' section of a post-build learning document. Be specific: name the exact Codex behavior discovered, the language feature encountered, or the workflow pattern observed. Do not write general lessons — write specific behaviors with file paths, function names, or API names." < build_notes.md

props.command: claude "Generate the 'What I learned' section. Be specific: name exact Codex behaviors, language features, or workflow patterns. Name file paths, function names, API names." < build_notes.md

beats.narration_text: learning_doc_generator.py — prompts user through 5-section form interactively, Claude generates the 'What I learned' and 'What I would do differently' sections with specific cited content, outputs formatted markdown.

props.code:
# interactive five-section post-build learning doc generator
import learning_engine, markdown_writer

build_notes = open('build_notes.md').read()
learned = learning_engine.generate_learned(build_notes)
would_change = learning_engine.generate_changes(build_notes)

doc = markdown_writer.format(sections=[
    ('What I built', build_notes),
    ('What I learned', learned),
    ('What I would do differently', would_change),
])
open('learning_doc.md', 'w').write(doc)

beats.narration_text: Generated 'What I learned' section: 4 specific entries. (1) Codex's default schema biases toward auto-increment integer keys — override with content-hash for idempotency. (2) AnimationPlayer must be a direct child of the node it animates — placing it as a sibling silently fails. (3) packed scene.instantiate() returns a copy. (4) delta accumulation needed for frame-independent motion. All four name exact behaviors.
Extract 'What I would do differently' section; use it to generate a new AGENTS.md lessons-learned entry.

'What I would do differently': 'write user needs with explicit viewport and font-size constraints for any phone-readable output.' Auto-generated AGENTS.md lessons entry: phone-readable outputs — specify max column count of 4 and minimum font size of 16px in user needs before running Code Mode. The post-build document closed the loop: build, reflection, permanent project memory.

The specificity requirement is the whole point. 'Learned about packed scenes' is not learnable — it's a category. 'packed scene.instantiate() returns a copy; mutations on the returned instance don't affect the packed resource' is learnable — it's a fact you can act on next time. Specificity is what separates a document you wrote from a document you can use.
Next: audit a Codex session for the five supervisory capacities.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist plus Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.
